outcome_text: Organizational practices are in place to enable AI testing, identification of incidents, and information sharing.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI testing and incident management infrastructure, including establishing testing environments and protocols, implementing AI incident identification and tracking mechanisms, and creating information-sharing practices for AI-related findings across organizational boundaries.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI testing and incident management infrastructure, including establishing testing environments and protocols, implementing AI incident identification and tracking mechanisms, and creating information-sharing practices for AI-related findings across organizational boundaries.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and audit methods, including fairness metric selection, bias test design, explainability documentation standards, and human oversight protocols.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-4.3 (GOVERN) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.615, cosine: 0.668). Secondary: AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.666), AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.665).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.616). Shared vocabulary: [ai, knowledge, protocols]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [across, boundaries, creating, environments, establishing, findings] vs WIDAI terms [audit, bias, design, documentation, explainability, fairness] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.888 (P=0.888, R=0.888). Balanced P/R — the 31-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (83.8%). Semantic tension between "Knowledge of AI testing and incident management infrastructure, including establ" and "Knowledge of responsible AI principles and audit methods, including fairness met". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.668 > STS 0.616 (gap +0.053). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, knowledge, protocols] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.6%. Shared tokens: [ai, knowledge, protocols]. NIST AI RMF-only: [across, boundaries, creating, environments, establishing, findings]. WIDAI-only: [audit, bias, design, documentation, explainability, fairness].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 83.8% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.616 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
